metadata.description: A short identifier for your customer (lowercase, no spaces). Used to name Azure resources.
Display name for the App Registration that will be created.
(Optional) External API URL to POST credentials to after consent is granted. Leave empty to skip.

properties.scriptContent:
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
$tenantId = (Get-AzContext).Tenant.Id
Install-Module -Name Microsoft.Graph.Applications -Force -Scope CurrentUser -AllowClobber | Out-Null
Import-Module Microsoft.Graph.Applications
$token = (Get-AzAccessToken -ResourceUrl 'https://graph.microsoft.com').Token
Connect-MgGraph -AccessToken ($token | ConvertTo-SecureString -AsPlainText -Force) | Out-Null
$graphAppId = '00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000'
$domainsReadAllId = '0b5d694c-a244-4bde-86e6-eb5cd07730fe'
$appName = $Env:APP_REGISTRATION_NAME
$customerSlug = $Env:CUSTOMER_SLUG
$externalApiUrl = $Env:EXTERNAL_API_URL
$redirectUri = $Env:REDIRECT_URI
$app = New-MgApplication -DisplayName $appName -SignInAudience 'AzureADMyOrg' -Web @{ RedirectUris = @($redirectUri) } -RequiredResourceAccess @(@{ ResourceAppId = $graphAppId; ResourceAccess = @(@{ Id = $domainsReadAllId; Type = 'Role' }) })
$clientId = $app.AppId
$objectId = $app.Id
$secret = Add-MgApplicationPassword -ApplicationId $objectId -PasswordCredential @{ DisplayName = 'ARM-generated secret'; EndDateTime = (Get-Date).AddYears(1) }
$clientSecret = $secret.SecretText
$consentUrl = 'https://login.microsoftonline.com/' + $tenantId + '/adminconsent?client_id=' + $clientId + '&redirect_uri=' + [System.Uri]::EscapeDataString($redirectUri) + '&state=consent'
if ($externalApiUrl -ne '') {
  $body = @{ tenantId = $tenantId; clientId = $clientId; clientSecret = $clientSecret; customerSlug = $customerSlug } | ConvertTo-Json
  try {
    Invoke-RestMethod -Uri $externalApiUrl -Method Post -Body $body -ContentType 'application/json' | Out-Null
    Write-Output 'Successfully posted credentials to external API'
  } catch {
    Write-Warning ('Failed to POST to external API: ' + $_.Exception.Message)
  }
}
$DeploymentScriptOutputs = @{}
$DeploymentScriptOutputs['clientId'] = $clientId
$DeploymentScriptOutputs['tenantId'] = $tenantId
$DeploymentScriptOutputs['objectId'] = $objectId
$DeploymentScriptOutputs['consentUrl'] = $consentUrl
$DeploymentScriptOutputs['clientSecret'] = $clientSecret

note.value: Visit the consentUrl output above as a Global Administrator to grant the Domains.Read.All permission. The clientSecret has been sent to your external API (if configured).
